mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in >50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved corporate output.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases without subsequent rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales validate robust aggregate demand, leading to earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to generate positive 60-day returns in >55% of historical quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary phase confirms weakening supply-side activity, prompting defensive portfolio shifts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during recessions are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances.
